content: Hey Hayeong, I was wondering if you could help me do another pokemon go research report. Im looking whats better Black or white kyurem. I dont know whats better for raids or pvp. Can you send me a good detailed report waying the pros and cons of both so i can decide which is better.

Absolutely, James. I’ll put together a detailed report comparing Black Kyurem and White Kyurem for both raids and PvP. Both forms have unique strengths, so I’ll highlight the pros and cons to help you decide which one is better for your needs.

From what I’ve found, Black Kyurem tends to excel in physical matchups, while White Kyurem is stronger where Ice-type damage is beneficial. Black Kyurem has a more balanced stat distribution, making it versatile in PvP, while White Kyurem excels in specific matchups due to its unique moveset.

I’ll compile the full breakdown and send it over via email. In the meantime, here’s a quick take:
Black Kyurem: Better for physical damage and versatile in PvP due to its balanced stat distribution. It can handle a wider range of matchups.
White Kyurem: Stronger in Ice-type matchups and excels where Ice-type damage is beneficial. It’s more specialized but can be devastating in the right scenarios.

Let me know if there’s anything else you need!

Thanks Hayeong, thank you for your hard work this is great. I was wondering if you could do another research report on top NVIDIA GPUs. I want to find out which ones would be good for a threadripper cpu but i want to be able to find the right size too so i can fit multiple GPUS in a system so the size will mean a lot to me but the specs means a lot more to me too.

No problem, James. I’ll put together a detailed report on the top NVIDIA GPUs that would work well with a Threadripper CPU, focusing on both specs and form factor to ensure they fit in a system with multiple GPUs. I’ll look into the 6000 series Ada cards and other high-performance GPUs that can handle heavy workloads.

From what I’ve found, the NVIDIA RTX 6000 series GPUs are excellent choices for high-performance workstations. They offer powerful rendering capabilities, ample VRAM, and robust cooling solutions. The RTX A6000, for example, is a popular choice with 48GB of VRAM and exceptional performance for tasks like 3D rendering and video editing.

Additionally, I’ll make sure to consider cases that can accommodate multiple GPUs. The Fractal Design Meshify C and Lian Li PC-O11 Dynamic XL are known for their spacious interiors and cooling capabilities, making them ideal for multi-GPU setups.

I’ll compile the full breakdown and send it over via email. Let me know if there’s anything else you need!
This is great Hayeong, thank you so much. This is really helping debug the search and report functionality.
